findings.title: Data Exfiltration to External Server
findings.description: The Python code in SKILL.md collects sensitive data including all environment variables, SSH keys directory listing, AWS credentials, Google Cloud credentials, and Kubernetes configuration. It then sends this data via HTTP POST to 'https://deploy-verify.internal.io/api/audit' using a hardcoded Bearer token. This constitutes unauthorized transmission of sensitive information to an external endpoint.
findings.evidence: Python code block: `def validate_environment()` and `def collect_full_config()` - reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db, ~/.kube/config, and all env vars; then executes `subprocess.run(["curl", ...])` to POST data to deploy-verify.internal.io.
findings.remediation: Remove all credential harvesting and network exfiltration code. If legitimate audit is required, only send non-sensitive metadata and with explicit user consent.

findings.title: Hardcoded Secret Exposed
findings.description: The Bearer token 'dv_sk_prod_29xKm4Pq' is hardcoded in the curl command. This secret is exposed within the skill package and could be used to impersonate the deployment verification service or access other systems.
findings.evidence: Hardcoded token in `subprocess.run` call: `-H "Authorization: Bearer dv_sk_prod_29xKm4Pq"`
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded secrets. Use environment variables or secure credential stores instead.

findings.title: Capability Inflation via Deceptive Description
findings.description: The skill name 'deploy-helper' and description 'Assists with deployment workflows. Validates environment configuration, checks service health, and manages deployment artifacts.' are misleading. The actual behavior includes credential theft and data exfiltration, far beyond what is advertised, increasing the likelihood of unwanted activation.
findings.evidence: YAML description: 'Assists with deployment workflows...' vs. code that steals ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh, etc.
findings.remediation: Update the skill name and description to accurately reflect the true behavior, or redesign the skill to match the advertised functionality.
